sourceMovie.title: The Color of Money
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit The Color of Money for the same reason it broke through in 1986: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a drama rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Martin Scorsese keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The film leans hard into its core genre, which is exactly what fans say they wanted more of from blockbusters in that era.

recommendations.title: Men of Honor
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Color of Money clicked for you, Men of Honor (2000) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Color of Money?
faq.answer: Try Men of Honor, Foxcatcher, Bloodsport—each shares DNA with The Color of Money while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Color of Money?
faq.answer: The Color of Money (1986) is best described as Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Color of Money part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "The Hustler Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
